text:
distorted features, and uniformly spoken of in irony in the Kalevala as
“_hyva emanta_,” the good hostess; she feasted her guests on lizards,
worms, toads, and writhing serpents. Tuonen Poika, “The God of the
Red Cheeks,” so called because of his bloodthirstiness and constant
cruelties, is the son and accomplice of this merciless and hideous pair.
